hook.text: John Little proved a simple formula in 1961: items in system = arrival rate × time in system.

buildup.text: Applied to Kanban: WIP = throughput × cycle time. Change one, you change them all.

discovery.text: Want faster delivery? Reduce WIP. The math is unavoidable.

twist.text: Little's Law is a universal truth — it applies to queues at a coffee shop, not just factories.

climax.text: One equation explains why doing fewer things at once gets them done faster.

punchline.text: Do less. Finish more. It's just math.

quiz.question: What does Little's Law tell us about reducing WIP?
options: Reducing WIP reduces cycle time, delivering faster
WIP has no effect on delivery speed
